questions.question: Is the harness menu rendered with three entries in the documented order (claude-code first, codex second, opencode third) and stable labels matching what the user reads in the spec? Does the menu use the existing `_menu_select` helper (no new menu primitive)?

Does wizard.py import HARNESS_NAMES from clauded.config (single source of truth) rather than hardcoding the tuple, so a future addition of a fourth harness automatically updates the menu? Does the menu position 0 always correspond to 'claude-code'?

Does the auto-add behaviour fire ONLY when chosen harness == 'opencode' AND 'opencode' not already in answers['frameworks']? Is the info-level message emitted exactly once and only when the auto-add actually adds the framework (no double message on a config that already has opencode)?

Are the three menu-shape tests written with recording-select-side-effect (capture the items list passed to _menu_select)? Do they assert the three menu values are exactly ('claude-code', 'codex', 'opencode') in that order, AND that the default_index equals HARNESS_NAMES.index(config.harness) for run_edit?

Does the auto-add test (AC-010) start from a config that lacks 'opencode' in frameworks and assert the resulting Config.frameworks contains 'opencode' AFTER the wizard returns? Is there a paired negative case asserting NO duplicate is added when 'opencode' is already in frameworks?

Does wizard.run() prompt for harness selection AFTER the frameworks step? Does the menu show three entries (Claude Code, Codex, opencode) with claude-code as the default cursor position for a fresh project (no existing config)?

After the user picks 'opencode' as harness in the wizard with answers['frameworks'] = ['playwright'] (missing opencode), does the resulting Config.frameworks contain 'opencode' (alongside the always-on claude-code/codex defaults and the chosen 'playwright')? Is an info-level message emitted explaining the auto-add?

Does wizard.run_edit() pre-select the existing harness via default_index = HARNESS_NAMES.index(config.harness)? With a config of harness='codex' and an unchanged menu choice, does the saved config still have harness='codex'?
Does the wizard's harness step avoid persisting the answer outside Config.from_wizard? Specifically, no env-var fallback, no global state mutation. The chosen value flows through answers dict only.
Does apply_detection_to_config (the non-interactive merge flow) PRESERVE config.harness without prompting and without dropping it? A detect-only run must not silently revert harness to the default.

Implementation note: a single shared helper `wizard._apply_harness_to_answers(answers, chosen)` performs both the harness-write and the auto-add-to-frameworks side effect. Both wizard.run/run_edit and wizard_integration.run_with_detection/run_edit_with_detection import and call this helper. The HARNESS_NAMES tuple is imported from clauded.config — single source of truth across the codebase.

Implementation note: apply_detection_to_config (line ~492) was missing harness from its Config(...) reconstruction; this would have silently reset harness to 'claude-code' on any detect-only update. Story 03 fixes this by adding `harness=config.harness` to the Config constructor call. Test test_apply_detection_to_config_preserves_harness in test_detect_integration.py guards against regression.

Implementation note: existing tests in test_wizard_distro.py used iterator-style mock_menu_select.side_effect = ["alpine", "3.12"] which now needed an extra 'claude-code' element for the harness step. All 8 such tests updated. The fix is mechanical and does not alter the original test intent.

Implementation note: the harness menu items are defined as a module-level constant `_HARNESS_MENU_ITEMS` in wizard.py, mapping display labels ('Claude Code' / 'Codex' / 'opencode  [adds opencode to frameworks]') to the same strings as HARNESS_NAMES. The opencode label deliberately includes the auto-add hint so the user knows what will happen before picking it.

Implementation note: the auto-add side effect emits 'opencode added to frameworks (required by harness selection).' via click.echo only when the auto-add actually appends the framework (i.e. it was not already present). Re-running with opencode already in frameworks is a no-op (no duplicate, no second message).
